scope: Every registered driver passes one exported behavioural conformance suite (internal/driver/drivertest.Run), and the assembled `sworn` loop boots end-to-end over a hermetic fixture release with a stub driver from a COLD board (internal/run.TestLoopSIT) — so a contract-violating driver or a dead loop wiring fails a test in CI instead of shipping a DOA release. Folds in the sworn#93 verified-path commit fix (internal/run/slice.go), whose regression assertion is AC-06.

test_results.command: go test -count=1 -timeout 300s ./... (full suite — 47 packages ok, 0 FAIL)
gofmt -l on changed .go files (empty) + go vet ./internal/run/ ./internal/driver/...

reachability.evidence: internal/run/loop_sit_test.go:TestLoopSIT boots the REAL RunParallel -> RunTrack -> RunSlice path (Router nil -> the production router auto-constructs and re-reads COMMITTED track-ref state via git; RunSliceFn is the real run.RunSlice wired only with an offline stub registry — not a mocked leaf) over the hermetic docs/release/sit-fixture release in internal/run/testdata/sit-fixture from a COLD board (release+track worktrees do NOT pre-exist; the fixture's release_worktree_path points at a non-existent temp path so RunParallel/RunTrack genuinely `git worktree add` them). Live run 2026-07-10: 'materialising release worktree' + '[T1-sit] materialising worktree' (AC-03 worktrees), router 'S01-sit-slice -> verify', 'generating design TL;DR' + 'running captain design-review' + 'implementing' + 'verifying (agentic)' + 'verdict PASS' (AC-03 captain/implement/verify legs fire through the registry; DoR gate passes on the first pass), then router 'verified -> merge-release' -> '[T1-sit] PAUSED' (D7: MergeTrackFn nil, a pause is the expected terminal). The test then reads the status.json COMMITTED on track/sit-fixture/T1-sit via `git show` and asserts state == verified (AC-06 — the committed ref, never the worktree file).
reachability.teeth_demo: AC-06 non-tautology proof (2026-07-10): reverting the verified-path repo.Stage(statusPath)+repo.Commit in internal/run/slice.go and re-running TestLoopSIT => '--- FAIL: TestLoopSIT (8.15s) ... AC-04: SIT loop STALLED — RunParallel did not return within 8s. The committed track ref never advanced to a terminal state, so the router re-dispatched forever (sworn#93 verified-commit regression).' The board dump showed the committed status.json stuck at "state": "implemented" and the router logged 'S01-sit-slice -> verify' dozens of times (the spin). slice.go was then restored via `git checkout` and the SIT passes again (0.29s). Captured in journal.md.
reachability.gate_output: The model-backed `sworn verify` gate could not produce a meaningful verdict in this environment. Attempt 1 (default verifier): `sworn verify ... -> 'model: SWORN_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set'` (no provider key / proxy login configured). Attempt 2 (keyless subprocess, the route verified sibling S05 used): `sworn verify --agentic --verifier-model claude-cli/sonnet ... -> {"verdict":"INCONCLUSIVE","failed_gate":"verifier_structured_unsupported","rationale":"verifier driver does not support structured output (ADR-0011)"}` — the keyless CLI driver cannot emit verifier-verdict-v1 here. Same environment class as S05. The canonical adversarial gate is the fresh-context /verify-slice handoff (Rule 7); the deterministic first-pass additionally carries the known spec-v1 'spec.md missing' false-FAIL (S10 has spec.json, no spec.md — no spec.md manufactured, per project guidance).

delivered.item: AC-01: exported conformance suite internal/driver/drivertest with entry Run(t, NewDriver) asserting the S01 behavioural contract clauses for ANY Driver — well-formed Result on success (Status set, tokens/duration non-negative, ResultText/StructuredJSON per role); every error path returns Status=error + non-empty ErrKind and never panics; an undeclared Role fails at the RESOLUTION boundary (Registry.Resolve role-arm, per the S01 Type-1 fail-fast-at-resolution decision amended into AC-01 @32328e5); a verifier-role dispatch returns StructuredJSON that parses as a JSON object or fails closed; the Rule-11 guard fires on a missing/non-worktree WorktreeRoot before any work

AC-02: the suite runs against all four compiled-in drivers (claude subprocess via fake `claude` binary, codex subprocess via fake `codex` binary, in-process oai + in-process responses via httptest through the S06 SWORN_PROXY_URL route) PLUS the reference StubDriver, via conformance_all_test.go iterating DefaultRegistry with FAIL-CLOSED enrolment (a registered driver name absent from the test enrolment map fails the suite)

AC-03: TestLoopSIT boots the assembled parallel loop (real RunParallel from cmd wiring, not a mocked leaf) over the hermetic sit-fixture release from a cold board with the StubDriver registered for all roles, and asserts: release+track worktrees materialise, implement+verify+captain dispatches fire through the registry (stub.RoleCounts), the verdict is consumed by the state machine, the slice reaches verified, and the process never panics
AC-04: (a) a driver conformance failure names the driver AND the clause via subtest naming (driver/clause), and (b) a stalled SIT fails with a bounded-deadline board-state dump rather than hanging
delivered.evidence: internal/driver/drivertest/conformance.go t.Run(clause) subtests under a per-driver parent; internal/run/loop_sit_test.go sitDeadline ctx + the ctx.Err()==DeadlineExceeded branch calling sitBoardDump — EXERCISED live in the teeth demo (STALLED at 8s with the git log + committed status.json dump)

delivered.item: AC-05: the whole slice runs with no network access or paid dispatch (fake binaries, httptest, transport-less stub only), and `go test ./internal/driver/... ./internal/run/...` passes; full `go test ./...` is green as the release-level backstop

AC-06 (sworn#93 regression): the SIT asserts the slice's `verified` transition is COMMITTED to the track ref (read via `git show track/.../status.json`), so a router re-read observes verified and does NOT re-dispatch verify; and it is DEMONSTRATED non-tautological — reverting the verified-path repo.Stage/repo.Commit makes the SIT stall to its bounded deadline with the committed ref stuck at implemented
delivered.evidence: internal/run/loop_sit_test.go:sitCommittedState + the AC-06 assertion + the verify-count bound; teeth_demo above + journal.md

delivered.item: sworn#93 fix present and correct: internal/run/slice.go PASS branch stages+commits the verified status write (matching every sibling terminal path); recorded as a Type-1 design_decision citing captain-proceed.md's Supplementary Coach decision as the human decision
delivered.evidence: internal/run/slice.go 'chore(run): slice verified — verdict consumed by state machine' commit @108c945; status.json design_decisions[7] (stake_class Type-1, architecturally_significant true)

delivered.item: Pin 2 (Coach disposition): the cold-board fixture is DoR-complete — intake.md need N-01 + release goal, spec.md ACs citing N-01 + a required test, a human-ratified validation record in status.json, and a stub that scripts the captain design-TL;DR + zero-escalate review + reqverify PASS legs — so implement.Run's Definition-of-Ready gate (RTM + reqverify + reqvalidate) passes on the FIRST pass, not via retry-bypass
delivered.evidence: internal/run/testdata/sit-fixture/{intake.md,S01-sit-slice/spec.md,S01-sit-slice/reqverify.md}; loop_sit_test.go sitStub (systemPrompt branch to sitReqverifyResults) + the Validation record in setupSITFixture; live run shows attempt 1 goes straight from captain-review to verifying with no DoR failure

not_delivered.item: Real-provider / real-CLI dispatch tests (the SIT and conformance suite run entirely offline — fake binaries, httptest, transport-less stub)
not_delivered.reason: Declared out_of_scope in spec.json: the Rule-10 release-cutover journey walk owns real-infra validation. The SIT proves the WIRING boots from a cold board; it does not and must not spend paid dispatch in CI.
not_delivered.acknowledgement: Brad (Coach), captain-proceed.md — out_of_scope ratified in the spec

divergence: SIT terminal decision is merge-RELEASE (not merge-track): the single-track single-slice fixture means that once S01-sit-slice verifies, the WHOLE release is terminal, so routeVerified -> routeMergeDecision returns merge-release. Either way the track PAUSES (MergeTrackFn nil, D7) and the committed-state assertion (verified) is unaffected. The spec text implies 'merge-track'; the observed pause reason is merge-release — a cosmetic consequence of a one-track fixture, not a wiring defect.

AC-03/AC-06 use the transport-less drivertest.StubDriver (design D4), so Coach disposition 3's exported-seam escape hatch was NOT needed for the SIT — the stub has no transport to wire. The in-process drivers' conformance legs (AC-02) use the landed S06 SWORN_PROXY_URL route (wiring.go ProxyEnrolments), adding NO exported production seam. Disposition 3's 'if the proxy route proves insufficient, an exported seam is a declared divergence' branch never triggered.

The fixture starts S01-sit-slice at `implemented` (a slice a prior /implement-slice finished, awaiting verify) rather than `planned`. Both are valid cold-board states (no worktrees). `implemented` is the EXACT sworn#93 shape: the first router decision is `verify`, so without the fix the spin is a pure verify re-dispatch, faithfully reproducing the bug.

The fixture ships status.json with start_commit="" so RunSlice self-bootstraps it to the track-branch HEAD (the cold-start path), rather than baking a commit SHA into testdata; board.json is copied with a __RELEASE_WT__ token the test substitutes with a temp path.

The deterministic first-pass / model-backed `sworn verify` gate could not produce a meaningful verdict here: no provider key / proxy login (see reachability.gate_output). Same environment class as verified sibling S05. The canonical gate is the fresh-context /verify-slice handoff. No spec.md was manufactured for the spec-v1 'spec.md missing' first-pass false-FAIL, per project guidance.

Touchpoint expansion (prior session @be5b9a2): the drivertest package also contains internal/driver/drivertest/stub.go (the exported StubDriver, design D4) and internal/driver/drivertest/wiring.go (the AC-02 fixture wiring — fake claude/codex binaries + the in-process SWORN_PROXY_URL enrolments), beyond the two drivertest files spec.json touchpoints literally enumerate (conformance.go, conformance_test.go). Both are squarely within 'the exported conformance suite' package this slice owns and are described in design.md (D4); the spec's touchpoint list simply did not enumerate every file of the new package. No file outside internal/driver/drivertest/, internal/driver/conformance_all_test.go, internal/run/{slice.go,loop_sit_test.go}, or internal/run/testdata/sit-fixture/ was touched.
